title: M6 closeout and future M7 entry prompt
summary: Close M6 after all product-gate tasks are independently reviewed and integrated, then write a non-executable future M7 entry prompt without starting M7.
acceptance: Closes M6 only after all M6 tasks are done and independently reviewed
Records Web tier verdict, Windows external-test verdict, performance and memory baselines, save/import/export results, blockers and M7 readiness
Writes coordination/reports/WM-0097-future-m7-entry-prompt.md as a non-executable future M7 prompt
Does not create, promote, claim, implement or review M7

history.details: Instantiated from reviewed WM-0084 M6 DAG; keep proposed until WM-0096 is done.

Drafted M6 closeout report, future M7 entry prompt and roadmap/project-state closeout markers; WM-0085 through WM-0096 are done; required checks pass including quality, m5-invariants, isolated pnpm bench and 100000-tick M5 headless.

verified: No blocking findings; verified WM-0085 through WM-0096 are done/reviewed/integrated, WM-0097 keeps M7 unstarted, records Roadmap-authoritative M6/M7/M8 scope, preserves Web demo-only and bounded unsigned Windows controlled-external-test verdicts, records required evidence/checks including isolated bench rerun and earlier concurrent bench failure, and keeps later owner gates intact.
Integrated verified M6 closeout after final gate: validate-handoff, taskctl validate/status, git diff --check, pnpm quality, m5-invariants, isolated pnpm bench and 100000-tick M5 headless all passed.

objective: Close M6 Web / Windows Product Gate, record all verdicts and evidence, and prepare the future M7 entry prompt without starting M7.
forbiddenPaths: M7 task creation promotion claim implementation or review
privacy policy commitments beyond readiness prompt
risksAndFileOwnership: Risk: M7 readiness prompt becomes M7 implementation.
Risk: closeout hides a missing M6 gate.
File ownership is limited to closeout report, future M7 prompt and narrow roadmap/project-state markers.
ownerGate: Do not create, promote, claim or implement M7
Block if any M6 task remains unverified or any required gate is missing
Block if final M6 verdict requires owner approval and approval is absent
m0M5RegressionCoverage: Final M6 closeout must preserve or explicitly migrate all prior milestone baseline evidence
M5 final world/read-model hashes 0xfba70a5c / 0x9ba83cb7 remain protected unless reviewed migration changes them
benchmarkImpact: Final M6 gate verdict cites benchmark evidence and preserves existing threshold policy.
reviewRouting: project-director completes to reviewer; reviewer verifies all M6 tasks are done and no M7 work was started.
rollbackModel: Leave M6 open, revert closeout/future prompt markers and do not promote M7.

sparkEligibility.reason: Milestone closeout, product verdict and future M7 prompt are project-director work and not Spark-eligible.

review.summary: No blocking findings; verified WM-0085 through WM-0096 are done/reviewed/integrated, WM-0097 keeps M7 unstarted, records Roadmap-authoritative M6/M7/M8 scope, preserves Web demo-only and bounded unsigned Windows controlled-external-test verdicts, records required evidence/checks including isolated bench rerun and earlier concurrent bench failure, and keeps later owner gates intact.

integration.summary: Integrated verified M6 closeout after final gate: validate-handoff, taskctl validate/status, git diff --check, pnpm quality, m5-invariants, isolated pnpm bench and 100000-tick M5 headless all passed.
